DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFFS KATHLEEN BARISH, ANDREW BARISH'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA OR FOR PROTECTIVE ORDERS IS GRANTED AS DEFENDANT HAS WITHDRAWN THE SUBPOENA THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS MOTION. THE COURT DOES NOT KNOW THE CONTENTS OF THE NEW SUBPOENA, BUT COUNSEL ARE ADVISED THAT THE ORIGINAL SUBPOENA WAS OVERLY BROAD AND WOULD NOT BE APPROVED IF NOT PROPERLY NARROWED IN SCOPE AND BREADTH. DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION OFFERS NO PERSUASIVE ARGUMENTS TO SUPPORT THE BREADTH OF THE PRIOR SUBPOENA. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. HAVING CONCEDED THAT THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO SOME BASIS OF INDEMNITY, CROSS-DEFENDANT CANNOT OBTAIN SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ANY ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INDEMNITY IN THE CROSS COMPLAINT. IN ANY EVENT, CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF EXCLUDING LIABILITY ON ANY THEORY. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANDREW S. WERNER OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CESARI, WERNER AND MORIARTY, TEL. NO. 391-1113 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT PETER DOWNEY;
ATTY. DEVIN C. CORTEAU OF THE ROPERS, MAJESKI KOHN AND BENTLEY, TEL. NO. 543-4800 APPEARING FOR THE OPPOSING DEFENDANT MORTON;
ATTY. KAREN L. MOORE OF THE LOW, BALL & LYNCH LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 981-6630 APPEARING FOR THE BROOKS DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT PETER DOWNEY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OF CROSS-COMPLAINT OF STEVEN D. BROOKS INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE BROOKS 2006 CHARITABLE REMAINDER UNITRUST DATED 3/14/06; CATHERINE S.S. BROOKS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE BROOKS 2006 CHARITABLE REMAINDER UNITRUST DATED 3/14/06 FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, IMPLIED AND EXPRESS INDEMNITY AND CONTRIBUTION IS DENIED. DOWNEY COULD BE FOUND TO HAVE BREACHED A DUTY TO PLAINTIFF'S TO THE EXTENT DAMAGE FROM DOWNEY'S ALLEGEDLY NEGLIGENT WORK OR INSPECTION FIRST AROSE OR WAS DISCOVERED AFTER PLAINTIFF'S PURCHASE, GIVEN THAT CROSS-COMPLAINT COULD LIKEWISE BE FOUND LIABLE ON A THEORY OF NEGLIGENCE AND GIVEN THAT DOWNEY HAS NOT SHOWN THAT ITS WORK WAS NOT NEGLIGENT, AND GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF'S INJURY (BUYING A HOME WORTH LESS THAN THEY HAD REASON TO BELIEVE) IS INDIVISABLE. DOWNING HAS NOT SHOWN THAT EQUITABLE INDEMNITY CANNOT BE AVAILABLE TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. Cross-Complaint is to submit a proposed form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 = (301/PJB)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Orders To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar May-05-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Derrick Stafford, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Kathleen Barish, Andrew Barish
Thomas J. LoSavio, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Brooks
Devin Courteau, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Jay Morton

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiffs Kathleen Barish, Andrew Barish's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. The Defendants are ordered to respond to the interrogatories as they seek the identification of the persons most knowledgeable about the profits and financial condition of the defendants. The court finds that C.C. Section 3295 does not contemplate the discovery sought by the remaining interrogatories without a finding that Plaintiff has shown a substantial probability that Plaintiff will prevail on the claim of punitive damages. The statutory provisions that are relied upon by moving party allow subpoenas for trial to be issued. The framework of Section 3295 cannot be expanded to allow all of the responses requested simply to frame a trial subpoena with more specificity. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: Plaintiffs Kathleen Barish, Andrew Barish's Motion To Quash Subpoena is granted.
